fields.description:
## Why
Collapse the agent's monitoring to ONE call returning ONE typed outcome, so agents never correlate votes/labels/status ‚Äî and the "both votes green but conflicts" trap is handled inside the tool, not by every session.

## What
A project command (project CLI/script, NOT `rebar` core; may `import rebar` only for ticket ops) that requests a land and returns one typed terminal outcome + distinct exit code. Blocking `--wait` + single-shot `land-status`.

## Dependencies
Depends on **S1** (`sterilized-papery-horse`, 843b-801f-9ef9-442d) ‚Äî the `Autosubmit` label, which `land` sets ‚Äî and **S3** (`darksome-starry-sable`, 0525-6d71-8969-4512) ‚Äî the recorded outcome, the authoritative source this command reads before falling back to native votes.

## Scope
The agent-facing command + the outcome enum contract. Lives in project tooling, not `src/rebar`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `land <change> --wait` sets `Autosubmit`, blocks until terminal, returns one typed outcome as JSON + a **distinct exit code per outcome** per this table: `0 merged`, `1 needs_rebase`, `2 ci_failed`, `3 review_failed`, `5 abandoned`, `124 timed_out`, any other failure ‚Üí `error` (nonzero, distinct from the above).
- [ ] `land-status <change>` single-shot variant returns the same enum **plus a non-terminal `pending`** value while the bot is still driving (it does NOT set `Autosubmit` ‚Äî that is `land`'s job; this resolves the fire-and-forget-then-check-back flow: run `land <change>` once, poll `land-status` which returns `pending` until terminal).
- [ ] **`--wait` termination/timeout:** poll `GET /changes/{id}` every 30s; detect terminal status (MERGED / ABANDONED / a failing label); a Gerrit **`ABANDONED`** status maps to the terminal `abandoned` outcome with its own distinct exit code (`5`); overall timeout 30 min ‚Üí `timed_out` (exit `124`); transport errors are retried (bounded) before surfacing `error`.
- [ ] **Fallback precedence** for deriving the outcome: (1) the bot's recorded outcome (S3) wins if present; else derive from native Gerrit state ‚Äî `Verified -1` ‚Üí `ci_failed`, `LLM-Review -1` ‚Üí `review_failed`, MERGED ‚Üí `merged`, `ABANDONED` ‚Üí `abandoned`; if both `-1` are present with no bot record, `ci_failed` wins. **`needs_rebase` is NOT derivable from native state** ‚Äî a change behind-tip still reports `submittable=True` and returns a fast-forward-only "not fast-forward" error only on the submit *attempt*, so `needs_rebase` requires the bot's recorded outcome (S3); it never appears via native-state fallback.
- [ ] Command lives in PROJECT tooling (repo CLI/script), NOT `rebar` core; may `import rebar` only for ticket ops (e.g. close the ticket on merge). No Gerrit-specific landing logic in `src/rebar`.
- [ ] The outcome enum is documented as a **stable, versioned contract** at `docs/land-contract.md` ‚Äî listing the enum values, the exit-code table, and a `contract-version`; `land --help` cites it and ADR-0042 (created by S6 `broken-checkered-tiger`) references it. Agents depend only on this contract.
